prompt_rules: world-map grassland variation tiles, not close-up village grass
include broad color drift, subtle plains texture, sparse flowers, worn patches, and regional palette variation
avoid a single repeated grass stamp or high-frequency checkerboard texture

prompt:
Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, soft warm GOLDEN-HOUR sunlight, hand-painted Ghibli-like look. A LUSH PAINTERLY top-down VALLEY FLOOR GRASS tileset for a 2D action RPG — the goal is to eliminate visible tile seams, checkerboard repetition, and corner-density mismatch. Base hue: deep emerald and matcha green with subtle warm-yellow sunlit highlights, cool blue-green shadow tones beneath grass blades. Micro-texture: each 128x128 cell is internally subdivided into a 4x4 grid of 32px sub-cells; every sub-cell has its own independent small cluster of sharply-pointed sasa grass blades at slightly varying angles and heights, with no large blank patches and no single dominant motif that can be spotted when tiles repeat. COLOR DRIFT: adjacent tiles in the sheet drift very subtly — some cells lean half a shade warmer (lime-green), others half a shade cooler (deep jade) — so when placed as a checker the variation reads as natural ground tone shift rather than pattern. DENSITY UNIFORMITY: the four corners of every cell must be filled to the same visual density as the center — no lighter or sparser corner zone that creates a diamond halo when tiles are tiled. Scatter elements distributed across all 16 cells and all sub-cell positions: tiny single-petal white or pale-yellow wildflowers (tsuyukusa, yamabuki, odamaki), micro-clover leaf clusters (small trefoil, NOT large rounded), soft moss pads on thin pebbles, occasional darker shadow blade clusters. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: 16 cells in a clean 4x4 grid. Row 1 = four fully seamless interior grass variations, each filled edge-to-edge. Row 2 = four more seamless variations with slightly different dominant sub-cell cluster arrangement. Row 3 = four more variations with a mild brightness or hue shift, one cell per variation having a small flower group, one a moss patch, one a shadow cluster, one a pebble. Row 4 = four accent tiles: cell13 = a small white flower group on lush grass, cell14 = a denser moss-and-stone micro-scene, cell15 = a slender striped-stem clump of susuki grass (ornamental grass accent), cell16 = a bare golden-earth patch rimmed by grass (transition hint). Every cell seamlessly connects to every other cell on all four sides. No hard outlines between sub-cells, no text, no UI, no character.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
